items.title: Tim Cook is stepping down as CEO of Apple: Here’s a look at his 15-year legacy, from new products and services to China expansion
items.content: Cook, who joined Apple in 1998, succeeded Steve Jobs as CEO in 2011 and went on to transform Apple into a $4 trillion powerhouse.

items.title: Apple’s John Ternus will run one of the world’s most powerful companies; the job is a minefield
items.content: Apple's top job comes with almost unrivaled power and money, but it comes with plenty of baggage, too.

items.title: Apple’s Cal AI crackdown signals it’s still policing the App Store
items.content: Apple tells TechCrunch Cal AI was removed not for web payments alone, but for deceptive billing, manipulative tactics, and App Store rule violations.

items.title: Who is John Ternus, the incoming Apple CEO?
items.content: Starting on September 1, Ternus will lead one of the world's most valuable companies, but if you're not a dedicated Apple enthusiast, you've probably never heard of this man,

items.title: Microsoft is officially killing its Outlook Lite app next month
items.content: Launched in 2022, Outlook Lite is a lightweight version of the regular Outlook app, designed for Android phones with limited storage and regions with slower internet connectio

items.title: Microsoft is working on yet another OpenClaw-like agent
items.content: The new features would be geared toward enterprise customers, with better security controls than the famously risky open source OpenClaw agent.

items.title: WireGuard VPN developer can’t ship software updates after Microsoft locks account
items.content: The popular open source VPN maker is the second high-profile developer to say Microsoft locked his account without notifying him and is blocking their ability to send software

items.title: Anthropic’s New Mythos A.I. Model Sets Off Global Alarms
items.content: Mythos has triggered emergency responses from central banks and intelligence agencies globally, as Anthropic decides who has access to the powerful model.

items.title: Trump’s Fed Pick Faces Tough Task Shedding ‘Sock Puppet’ Label

items.title: The Consequences of SpaceX’s Latest A.I. Moonshot
items.content: Elon Musk’s rocket and satellite internet titan may pay $60 billion for an A.I. coding start-up. It further clouds SpaceX’s once-simple business plan.

items.title: Lufthansa Cuts 20,000 Flights to Save Fuel Amid Iran War Price Surge
items.content: Global jet fuel prices have jumped more than 70 percent since the start of the war, pressuring airlines in Europe, the largest consumers of jet fuel shipped through the Strait
